answer:
**Gāndharva marriage** (also called *Gāndharva vivāha*), defined across the Smṛti corpus as the consensual union of a maiden and her lover without parental involvement or formal rites, is the **fifth** of the traditional **eight forms of marriage** (*aṣṭau vivāhāḥ*). It arises from mutual desire (*kāma*) and has sexual union as its immediate purpose, distinguishing it from the first four orthodox forms (Brāhma, Daiva, Ārṣa, Prājāpatya), which emphasize parental gift-giving and ritual propriety.[^1][^2][^3][^4]

### Key Characteristics
- **Consent-based**: Performed by the voluntary agreement (*sambandhaḥ*) of a willing man (*svīkṛtaḥ puruṣaḥ*) and woman (*svīkṛtā strī*), often eloping lovers (*sambandhāt kanyakāyāḥ kāmajam*); no bride-price, abduction, or force.[^5][^6]
- **Status**: Orthodox (*dharmyaḥ*) for Kṣatriyas (*rājanyebhyaḥ*) and common (*sādhāraṇaḥ*) to all *varṇas*, but inferior to the first four; sons (*putra*) are valid heirs yet spiritually less purifying (e.g., fewer ancestral generations redeemed than from Brāhma unions).[^7][^8][^9]
- **Offspring**: From unblamed Gāndharva unions, progeny (*santanam*) is *guṇavān* (virtuous); blameworthy ones yield cruel, untruthful sons.[^10]
- **Restrictions**: Not ideal for Brāhmaṇas (who prefer first six forms); mixed with Rākṣasa, lawful for Kṣatriyas.[^11]

### Occurrences in the Corpus
The concept is standardized in major Smṛtis (no explicit mentions in Aṅgirasa, Parāśara, or Yama from available texts):

| Smṛti              | Definition & Status                                                                 | Citation |
|--------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------|
| **Manusmṛti** (Ch. 3) | Voluntary union (*svayam... sambandhād... gāndharvam vidyāt*) of lovers; fifth form; lawful for Kṣatriya (mixed OK); inferior sons.[^12] | [Ch. 3](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/03/translation/#p-beb0f2)[^13] |
| **Nāradasmṛti** (Ch. 1) | Mutual desire (*parasparaḥ saṃkalpo gāndharvaḥ pañcamo vidhiḥ*) of willing lovers; common to all; righteous like first four.[^14] | [Ch. 1](/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-687a38) |
| **Yājñavalkya Smṛti** (Ch. 1) | By mutual agreement (*gāndharvaḥ paraspara saṃkalpāt*); lists among eight forms.[^15] | [Ch. 1](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/#p-f1f83f) |
| **Viṣṇu Smṛti** (Ch. 24) | Union of lovers without parents (*gāndharvaḥ preyamāṇayor abhimānāt*); righteous, esp. for Rājanyas.[^16] | [Ch. 24](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/24/translation/#p-60e88f) |

**Kātyāyana Smṛti** implies it via *varṇasaṃkara* discussions but lacks explicit definition in wiki/orientation.[^17] No coverage in Aṅgirasa, Parāśara, Yama, or Bṛhaspati Smṛtis (per searches/wikis).

This form reflects romantic autonomy (*svayaṃvara*-like) but warns of spiritual/social risks, prioritizing orthodox unions for Brāhmaṇas.

[^1]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 3, [para.](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/03/translation/#p-b07c61).
[^2]: Nāradasmṛti, Ch. 1, [para.](/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-537f19).
[^3]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, Ch. 1, [para.](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/#p-f1f83f).
[^4]: Viṣṇu Smṛti, Ch. 24, [para.](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/24/translation/#p-3a34b6).
[^5]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 3, [para.](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/03/translation/#p-beb0f2).
[^6]: Nāradasmṛti, Ch. 1, [para.](/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-687a38).
[^7]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 3, [para.](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/03/translation/#p-a1ba3f).
[^8]: Viṣṇu Smṛti, Ch. 24, [para.](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/24/translation/#p-15dd77).
[^9]: Nāradasmṛti, Ch. 1, [para.](/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-3b1b4d).
[^10]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 3, [para.](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/03/translation/#p-df466e).
[^11]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 3, [para.](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/03/translation/#p-a1ba3f).
[^12]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 3, [full chapter](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/03/translation/).
[^13]: Ibid., [para.](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/03/translation/#p-b07c61).
[^14]: Nāradasmṛti, Ch. 1, [full chapter](/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/).
[^15]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, Ch. 1, [full chapter](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/).
[^16]: Viṣṇu Smṛti, Ch. 24, [full chapter](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/24/translation/).
[^17]: Kātyāyana Smṛti wiki (no explicit Gāndharva passages highlighted).
